title: Grigor Tansad

{{Quote|Today, Tansad reported a breakthrough — he confirmed that the pictographs correspond to dates. His finds were correlated between the beginning sounds each pictograph makes, Aaris III's annual revolution, and the overall pattern and number of the series of pictographs. Subsequently Jelok has abandoned his theories on the language and is now endeavoring to assist Tansad in deciphering the sounds.|From the [[Doctor Lancer Brunou's journal|journal]] of Doctor Lancer Brunou|<ref name="AOA" />}}
'''Grigor Tansad''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] who served in the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]]. In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], he was assigned to science team [[MS-133]] which was charged with [[Archaeology/Legends|excavating]] [[City of Kastays|extensive ruins]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aaris III]]. During the expedition, Tansad was responsible for discovering a [[Place of Kastays|chamber]] filled with [[pictograph]]s. Tansad theorized that the glyphs corresponded to a dating system used by the [[Aaris]], the native [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that had apparently gone extinct several thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior.

During the mission, Tansad's fellow scientist, [[Jelok]], discovered the [[Plaque of Victory]], a sentient inanimate artifact that sought to manipulate the beings around it by heightening their suspicions and hostility, eventually turning them against each other. As the team began to suspect that they were being watched from the jungle, they were attacked by the primitive descendants of the Aaris. Tansad was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the pictograph chamber by a blow to the head, and his body hidden under the rubble. Ultimately, only two members of the team, [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Lancer Brunou]] and research assistant [[Solla Deremot]], survived to be rescued by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which had answered a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] sent by Brunou.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Today our careful work paid off—Tansad discovered a way into one of the ruined metal structures in the field near the shuttle.|From the journal of Doctor Lancer Brunou|<ref name="AOA" />}}
[[File:Aaris_III.jpg|right|thumb|Aaris III]]
Grigor Tansad was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] assigned to science team [[MS-133]] under the leadership of [[Doctor/Legends|Doctor]] [[Lancer Brunou]]. Part of the [[Moff Sarne's forces|forces]] [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] of the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]], MS-133 was dispatched in [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]] to conduct an [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeological]] expedition to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aaris III]] to determine why the [[Aaris]]—the world's [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]—had apparently gone extinct several thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior. During the [[Standard month/Legends|months]]-long mission, Tansad assisted his fellow team members in excavating and cataloging [[City of Kastays|extensive ruins]]. On the forty-ninth [[Standard day/Legends|day]] of the mission, Tansad discovered an entrance into one of the [[Place of Kastays|ruined structures]]. A secondary base camp was established near to the building, and Tansad—along with fellow scientist [[Jelok]], two [[Technician/Legends|technicians]] and five [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]]—began exploring the chamber.<ref name="AOA" />

Inside the ruin, Tansad and Jelok discovered [[pictograph]]s that covered the walls and emitted sounds when touched. Jelok held the opinion that the glyphs were the key to the [[Language/Legends|language]] of the Aaris, but Tansad believed that they were, in fact, a pictorial dating system that recorded the civilization's history in an aural form. Both scientists worked on their own theories; after several days, Tansad discovered correlations between the beginning sound emitted by each pictograph, the annual revolution of Aaris III, and the pictograph's overall patterns and number. The breakthrough confirmed his theory, and Jelok abandoned his own work to assist Tansad in deciphering the designs. The next day, Jelok found a [[Plaque of Victory|metal ingot]] concealed in a floor safe while clearing rubble in the chamber. Doctor Brunou analyzed the artifact at the main base camp, and decided to send it to Moff Sarne's research facilities on his [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], for further testing when he discovered that the ingot was composed of an unknown material and radiating energy. The artifact, in fact, contained a sentient lifeform that fed off of the life-force of living beings by manipulating their emotions, raising their hostility and suspicions and turning them against one another. Known as the "Plaque of Victory" by the Aaris, the artifact's influence had been responsible for the [[Aaris civil war|civil war]] that had caused the downfall of the civilization thousands of years prior.<ref name="AOA" />

Shortly after the artifact was discovered, members of the team began reporting that they felt they were being watched from the jungle. Although there was no evidence of other living beings on Aaris III, the [[Unidentified Imperial lieutenant (MS-133)|Lieutenant]] in command of MS-133's military detachment and five [[Soldier/Legends|troopers]] entered the jungle to investigate. [[Blaster/Legends|Blaster]] fire was heard an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] later, and the soldiers did not return to camp. Brunou considered recalling the expedition, but Tansad and Jelok continued their work in the chamber. Tansad continued to decipher the pictographs and enter his findings into his [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]]. He translated eleven rows of pictographs, learning that the Aaris had been an industrial society that had raised large [[City/Legends|cities]] and were relatively peaceful, although they had fought occasional wars. He learned that the author of the recordings was a scholar by the name of [[Kastays]], and that some of the pictographs chronicled the last days of the Aaris as they descended into civil war. Kastays' recordings described how the Plaque of Victory was discovered and the effect it had rendered on the population of the city, slowly turning them against each other before compelling them to erupt into full blown conflict. In a final effort to negate its influence, the artifact was sealed in a floor vault in Kastays' chamber high above the city. As Tansad noted his discoveries, he also commented that he had a feeling that something was terribly wrong on the planet, and that Jelok was jealous of his discoveries.<ref name="AOA" />

[[File:Place_of_Kastays.jpg|thumb|left|Members of the ''FarStar'' crew find Tansad's body in the Place of Kastays.]]
Shortly after, Tansad was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the pictograph chamber. His [[Bone/Legends|skull]] was crushed by a heavy blow with a fist-sized rock, and his body was hidden under the rubble. The remaining members of MS-133 were unaware of his death, and Jelok only reported that he was missing from the chamber to Brunou. Following Tansad's death, MS-133 was attacked<ref name="AOA" /> by the primitive descendants of the Aaris.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|154|Jelok}}</ref> Several assaults took place, and eventually the team was reduced to two members, Brunou and Research Assistant [[Solla Deremot]]. The pair were rescued by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which had responded to a [[Distress signal/Legends|distress call]] that Brunou had sent to Kal'Shebbol, unaware that the planet had [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|fallen]] to the New Republic in the intervening months. The crew of the ''FarStar'' conducted an investigation into the events that had occurred on the planet, and discovered Tansad's body in the pictograph chamber, along with the rock used to kill him covered in [[Blood/Legends|blood]].<ref name="AOA" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Despite feelings that something here is terribly wrong (or is it simply Jelok's jealousy arousing my suspicions?), I am slowly discovering what happened in this ancient place.|Grigor Tansad notes|<ref name="AOA" />}}
Grigor Tansad was a scientist with experience in alien linguistics, able to make reasoned hypotheses and correlate findings to prove theories. During the Aaris III expedition, Tansad's theory on the nature of the Aaris pictographs proved to be correct. Despite this, he had an unnerving feeling that something was wrong on the planet during MS-133's time on Aaris III, and he believed that Jelok was jealous that his own theory regarding the pictographs was incorrect.<ref name="AOA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Grigor Tansad was introduced in the [[Roleplaying game|RPG adventure]] ''[[Artifact of Aaris]]'', published in ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[1995]]. The adventure implies that, under the influence of the Plaque of Victory the members of MS-133 turned upon each other, resulting in multiple deaths and the destruction of the team's [[Unidentified Curich-class shuttle|shuttle]].<ref name="AOA" /> However, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], confirmed that the team came under attack from the remnants of the Aaris civilization.<ref name="CSWE" />

Regardless, ''Artifact of Aaris'' implies that Tansad was murdered by Jelok. Tansad's own notes state that he felt that Jelok was jealous of his work, and Tansad's body was found under the rubble in the chamber in which he and Jelok were working. Tansad had been killed by a blow to the head, and the murder weapon was nearby, still covered in blood. However, since neither source directly confirms these implications, this article only presents the information without assumptions.<ref name="AOA" />

==Appearances==
*{{DarkStryder|adventure=Artifact of Aaris|book=The DarkStryder Campaign|reprint=yes}} {{1stm}}
